code: @Manual{,   title = {RcTools: A Collection of Report Card Tools},   author = {Adam Shand},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://add-am.github.io/RcTools/}, }

what: A Collection of Report Card Tools
title: A Collection of Report Card Tools
text: goal RcTools provide collection tools (functions) utilised Northern Three Report Cards’ technical staff. tools written bridge gap : Online data resources desktop analysis, Finish analysis products N3 technical reports. .e., tools focus tasks downloading data, converting values scores, converting scores grades, producing stylised visual products plots, maps, formatted xlsx files.

title: A Collection of Report Card Tools
text: install package first need download RTools package install computer (accept defaults everywhere installation process). Following , can install development version RcTools GitHub : Finally, load package just like R package:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("add-am/RcTools") library(RcTools)

title: A Collection of Report Card Tools
text: package currently contains 9 functions still expanding. Functions can grouped two themes: eReefs related functions Data table related functions Functions theme explained . Alternatively, individual function documentation can accessed command ?function_name.

title: A Collection of Report Card Tools
text: ereefs_extract function takes geometry input, date, variable, returns netCDF data object within bounds.
code: #first a geometry object is loaded in using the sf package sf_obj <- system.file("extdata/boundary.gpkg", package = "RcTools") sf_obj <- sf::st_read(sf_obj)  #then the extract function can be run nc <- ereefs_extract(   Region = sf_obj, #define the region to look at with the sf object   StartDate = "2022-03-01",   EndDate = "2022-03-03",   Variable = "Turbidity",   Downsample = 0 #downsample reduces the size of the data by taking every nth cell )

title: A Collection of Report Card Tools
text: ereefs_reproject function takes curvilinear netCDF object returns regular grid netCDF object. curvilinear netCDF object generally produced ereefs_extract function. Note, function tends fail curvilinear netCDF object complex border, edge Mackay Whitsunday Isaac region.
code: #reproject the data nc <- ereefs_reproject(nc)

title: A Collection of Report Card Tools
text: ereefs_dotplot function takes regular grid netCDF object returns dotplot summarising daily values, overall mean, rolling average. product function looks like :
code: #plot data p <- ereefs_plot(   nc = nc,   Heading = "Dry Tropics",   YAxisName = "Chlorophyll a (mg/m3)",   LogTransform = TRUE ) p

title: A Collection of Report Card Tools
text: ereefs_windplot function takes list regular grid netCDF objects (specifically four objects produced requesting wind ereefs_extract function) returns windrose plot summarising wind strength direction. product function looks like :
code: wr_p <- ereefs_windrose(   nc = nc_2,    SubSample = 500,    Heading = "Approximated Wind Speed",    LegendTitle = "Speed (km/h)" ) wr_p

title: A Collection of Report Card Tools
text: ereefs_map function takes either singular regular grid netCDF object, list regular grid netCDF objects returns one three different map types. Map ) concentration map data turbidity, map B) true colour map shows “true” colour water, map C) vector field map provides spatial representation wind strength direction. Note visuals true colour maps suffer temporal aggregation (instead short animations used). product function looks like :
code: m <- ereefs_map(   nc = nc,   MapType = "Concentration",   Aggregation = "Month",   LegendTitle = "Turbidity (NTU)",   nrow = 2 #the number of rows to use if/when maps are facetted ) m

title: A Collection of Report Card Tools
text: value_to_score() function designed calculate variety report card scores used Northern Three Report Cards. function takes dataframe appends additional column dataframe calculated scores. Currently function can score following indices/indicators: water quality indicators (freshwater, estuarine, marine environments) Mangroves saltmarsh Wetlands Riparian vegtation (freshwater estuarine) Fish Depending index/indicator different amount inputs requried. example use value_to_score() function calculate scores water quality indicators freshwater environment. First need table data: can call function just like tidyverse function. Importantly, function written “pipe-friendly” .e. can use function , pipe. say value_to_score(df, ...) df |> value_to_score(...): Note arguments provided quotes flexible (.e. can provided without quotes), adheres tidyverse principles. However, two arguments provided quotes flexible - must quotes. two arguments handled function internally different way. Another example function used score riparian vegetation time follows. Note scoring method lot less information required. Helpers notify required information missing. anycase, output function original table, now additional score column. name score column inherited name value column + “Score” added end - provides easy link back source scores. example table look like :
code: #load the package library(RcTools)  #create an example dataframe df <- data.frame(   WQIndicator = c(rep("DIN", 3), rep("Low DO", 3)),   WQObjective = c(rep(0.02, 3), rep(90, 3)),   WQScalingFactor = c(rep(0.38, 3), rep(70, 3)),   WQValue = c(0.002, 0.017, 0.029, 65, 93, 101) )  #calculate the twentieth and eightieth percentile values df <- df |>    dplyr::mutate(     WQEightieth = quantile(WQValue, probs = 0.8),     WQTwentieth = quantile(WQValue, probs = 0.2)   ) #run the scoring function df <- df |>    value_to_score(     value = WQValue,     value_type = "Water Quality",     water_type = "Freshwater",     indicator = WQIndicator,     wqo = WQObjective,     sf = WQScalingFactor,     eightieth = WQEightieth,     twentieth = WQTwentieth   ) #create an example dataframe df <- data.frame(   RiparianValue = c(1, 0, -0.1, -1, -3, -5) )  #run the scoring function. As a demonstration, no pipe has been used this time. df <- value_to_score(         df = df,         value = RiparianValue,         value_type = "Riparian"       )

title: A Collection of Report Card Tools
text: score_to_grade() function designed directly follow value_to_score() function. takes dataframe (scores) appends additional column dataframe calculated grades. Currently function provide grades adhere following range: 81 100 = 61 80 = B 41 60 = C 21 40 = D 0 20 = E Broadly speaking, majority grades follow range, range required function updated. basic example use score_to_grade() function. reason multiple columns contain scores, multiple columns can also provided: final output look like :
code: #create an example dataframe (duplicate of above table) df <- data.frame(   RiparianValue = c(1, 0, -0.1, -1, -3, -5),   RiparianValueScore = c(81.19, 80.90, 61.00, 21.00, 20.48, 20.06) )  #run the grading function. df <- score_to_grade(df, RiparianValueScore)

title: A Collection of Report Card Tools
text: save_n3_table() function designed take finalised dataframe within R (one ) save excel file formatting specific style used Northern Three Report Cards. basic example use save_n3_table() function format data frame Northern Three Report Card. use table just created example. first instance, apply styling score column: looks like :  also possible apply styling grade column well. However, side effect score column inherits grades well (formatting now done character value): looks like :
code: #run the "save as n3 table" function save_n3_table(   df = df,   file_name = "Riparian Scores and Grades",    target_columns = 2,    target_rows = 1:nrow(df),    scheme = "Report Card",   ) #run the "save as n3 table" function save_n3_table(   df = df,   file_name = "Riparian Scores and Grades",    target_columns = 2:3, #note we expanded the column range   target_rows = 1:nrow(df),    scheme = "Report Card",    include_letter = TRUE   )

title: A Collection of Report Card Tools
text: save_n3_table() offers several extensions styling choices, follows: Report Card (without letter grades): colours cell red green based standard Report Card boundaries. Demonstrated . Rainfall: colours cells brown blue based average rainfall codes (1 7). Temperature: colours cells blue red based average temperature codes (1 7). Summary Statistics: compares mean/median values WQOs colours cells blue pass orange fail. Presence Absence: colours cells based presence (blue) absence (grey), used fish observation data. extended styling options implimented way, scheme argument changing. styling choices follows: Rainfall:  Temperature:  Summary statistics:  Presence absence:

title: A Helper That Presents Conditional Formatting Options — conditional_formatter
text: data main dataframe run functions workbook excel workbook object. target_columns Numeric Vector. columns colour grading applied . e.g. 1:10. target_rows Numeric Vector. rows colour grading applied . e.g. 1:10. scheme String. Defines conditional formatting rules colours. Options Report Card Grade, Report Card Score, Rainfall Temperature, Summary Stat include_letter Boolean. letter grade attached score? specific Report Card scores file_name String. name file want save. Without extension

title: Plotting eReefs Data — ereefs_dotplot
text: nc single NetCDF (stars) object list NetCDF (stars) objects, generally produced extract_ereefs() function SubSample Numeric String. number values per day plot dot plot. Defaults 500 values per day. Heading Character String. heading plot. Defaults NULL. YAxisName Character String. yaxis label text plot. Defaults NULL returns "value" plot. LogTransform Boolean. data presented log transformation - useful variables Turbidity Chlorophyll AttributeName Character String. name attribute used several exist. Defaults NULL

title: Mapping eReefs Data — ereefs_map
text: nc single NetCDF (stars) object list NetCDF (stars) objects, generally produced extract_ereefs() function MapType Character String. Defines type map produced. One "Concentration", "True Colour", "Vector Field" (AKA wind) Aggregation Character String. Defines level aggregation apply. Defaults "Month". Options "Month", "Season", "Financial", "Annual" LegendTitle Character String. title legend. Defaults name netCDF's attribute LogScale Boolean. want data displayed log scale? nrow Numeric String. number rows used facetting maps. Defaults NULL uses underlying default value

title: Mapping eReefs Data — ereefs_map
text: tmap object

title: Mapping eReefs Data — ereefs_map
code: nc <- system.file("extdata/turb_reg.nc", package = "RcTools") nc <- stars::read_mdim(nc)  m <- ereefs_map(   nc = nc,   MapType = "Concentration",   Aggregation = "Annual" )

text: MinPath character string. Defines full path folder min temp sub folders stored. Generally user/.../.../raw/tmin2/ MaxPath character string. Defines full path folder max temp sub folders stored. Generally user/.../.../raw/tmax2/ CropObj Sf Object. sf object used define area data cropped . Generally, n3_region object build_n3_region() function used.

title: Extract, Crop, and Combine Air Temperature Data — extract_air_temp

title: Extracting eReefs Data — extract_ereefs
text: Extracting eReefs Data

title: Extracting eReefs Data — extract_ereefs
code: extract_ereefs(   Model = "catalog",   Region,   StartDate,   EndDate,   Variable,   Downsample = 0 )

title: Extracting eReefs Data — extract_ereefs
text: Model Character String. Describes url model use. Defaults "catalog" provides user options Region SF Object. sf object defining area extract data StartDate POSIX time. Supplied string format YYYY-MM-DD defines first day extract data EndDate POSIX time. Supplied string format YYYY-MM-DD defines last day extract data Variable Character Vector. variable extract eReefs, currently accessible: "Turbidity", "Chlorophyll ", "DIN", "NH4", "NO3", "Secchi", "pH", "Wind", "True Colour" Downsample Integer. singular value defines level downsampling apply data (reduces data size processing time, also reduces resolution)

title: Extracting eReefs Data — extract_ereefs
text: single NetCDF (stars) object specified dimensions attributes

title: Extracting eReefs Data — extract_ereefs
code: if (FALSE)  #dont run because function takes a while to execute and calls interactive component sf_obj <- system.file("extdata/boundary.gpkg", package = "RcTools") sf_obj <- sf::st_read(sf_obj) #> Error: object 'sf_obj' not found  nc <- extract_ereefs(   Region = sf_obj,   StartDate = "2022-03-01",   EndDate = "2022-03-03",   Variable = "Turbidity",   Downsample = 0 ) #> Error: object 'sf_obj' not found  # \dontrun{}

what: Extract AIMS MMP Logger Data — extract_logger
title: Extract AIMS MMP Logger Data — extract_logger
text: Extract AIMS MMP Logger Data

title: Extract AIMS MMP Logger Data — extract_logger
code: extract_logger(   Years,   Loggers,   Indicators = c("chlorophyll", "turbidity"),   FilterFlags = TRUE,   FlagTags = c(1, 2),   Aggregate = FALSE,   AggregationType,   SmallTables = FALSE,   RowCount = 1500 )

title: Extract AIMS MMP Logger Data — extract_logger
text: Years Numeric Vector. year(s) data want access. Loggers Character Vector. name(s) logger(s) want access. Indicators Character Vector. name(s) indicators want access, can Chlorophyll /Turbidity. FilterFlags Boolean. want filter data quality flags? defaults TRUE (yes). FlagTags Numeric Vector. vector flag tags keep, choices : 0 =  No_QC_performed, 1 = Good_data, 2 = Probably_good_data, 3 = Bad_data_that_are_potentially_correctable, 4 = Bad_data, 5 = Value_changed. Advice data providers keep tags 1 2. default tags 1 2 kept. Aggregate Boolean. want aggregate data daily values? defaults FALSE () returns 10-minute interval data. AggregationType Character String. Defines type aggregation apply, one : Hourly, Daily SmallTables Boolean. want return small tables (less 1,500 rows per table). defaults FALSE () RowCount Numeric String. number rows "small table", defaults 1500

title: Extract AIMS MMP Logger Data — extract_logger
text: list dataframes

title: Extract AIMS MMP Logger Data — extract_logger
code: wq_data <- extract_logger( Years = 2025,  Loggers = "BUR2" )

WaterLakes Boolean. lakes included map layer? Defaults TRUE. StreamOrder Numeric Vector length 1 2. Defines minimum, minimum maximum, stream order filter WaterLines paramter . 1 = smallest streams increasing numbers correspond increasing stream sizes. Defaults NULL ().

what: Create a Tmap Inset (corner) Map — maps_inset_layer
title: Create a Tmap Inset (corner) Map — maps_inset_layer
text: Create Tmap Inset (corner) Map

title: Create a Tmap Inset (corner) Map — maps_inset_layer
code: maps_inset_layer(   supplied_sf_1,   background,   aspect,   use_bbox_1 = TRUE,   supplied_sf_1_colour = "red",   background_colour = "white",   supplied_sf_2 = NULL,   use_bbox_2 = TRUE,   supplied_sf_2_colour = "blue" )

title: Create a Tmap Inset (corner) Map — maps_inset_layer
text: supplied_sf_1 Sf object. sf object defines main area interest. background Sf object. sf object defines greater region around area interest. example supplied_sf_1 collection water quality samples, background basin collected. parameter also defines zoom level map, .e. map size adjusted scale object. aspect Numeric String. numeric value describes aspect ratio parent map. Suggest range 0.8 1.2. ensures correct positioning inset map. use_bbox_1 Boolean. bounding box sf object used (TRUE) actual outline sf object (FALSE). Deafults TRUE. supplied_sf_1_colour Character String. character value changes colour supplied_sf_1 object. Defaults red. background_colour Character String. character value changes colour background object. Defaults white. supplied_sf_2 Sf object. sf object. sf object defines secondary area interest. Defaults NULL use_bbox_2 Boolean. bounding box sf object used (TRUE) actual outline sf object (FALSE). Deafults TRUE. supplied_sf_2_colour Character String. character value changes colour supplied_sf_2 object. Defaults blue.

title: Create a Tmap Inset (corner) Map — maps_inset_layer
text: Tmap Object

title: Create a Tmap Inset (corner) Map — maps_inset_layer
code: #load in required sf objects sf_obj_1 <- sf::st_read(system.file("extdata/ross.gpkg", package = "RcTools"), quiet = TRUE) sf_obj_2 <- sf::st_read(system.file("extdata/dry_tropics.gpkg", package = "RcTools"), quiet = TRUE)  example_inset <- maps_inset_layer(  supplied_sf_1 = sf_obj_1,   background = sf_obj_2,   aspect = 1.1 )

text: Crop Raster Increase Resolution Time

title: Crop A Raster and Increase its Resolution at the Same Time — nc_high_res_crop
code: nc_high_res_crop(nc, CropObj, DisaggFactor = 5)

title: Crop A Raster and Increase its Resolution at the Same Time — nc_high_res_crop
text: nc single NetCDF (stars) object, usually produced part Climate workflow. CropObj sf object defines area data cropped . DisaggFactor Numeric string. Defines level disaggregation apply data (.e. many additional cells create). Defaults five, .e. one cell become five cells.

title: Crop A Raster and Increase its Resolution at the Same Time — nc_high_res_crop
text: singele NetCDF (stars) object.

title: Crop A Raster and Increase its Resolution at the Same Time — nc_high_res_crop
code: nc <- stars::read_mdim(system.file("extdata/turb_reg.nc", package = "RcTools")) nc <- ereefs_reproject(nc) #> threshold set to 1415.03 : set a larger value if you see missing values where there shouldn't be area <- sf::st_read(system.file("extdata/example_box.gpkg", package = "RcTools")) #> Reading layer `example_box' from data source  #>   `/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/RcTools/extdata/example_box.gpkg'  #>   using driver `GPKG' #> Simple feature collection with 1 feature and 0 fields #> Geometry type: POLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 1517030 ymin: -2151353 xmax: 1619058 ymax: -2064030 #> Projected CRS: GDA94 / Australian Albers area <- sf::st_transform(area, sf::st_crs(nc))  nc_high_res <- nc_high_res_crop(nc, area, 5) #> Warning: no_data_value not set: missing values will appear as zero values

what: Split a Table into N rows — row_splitter

text: Split Table N rows

title: Split a Table into N rows — row_splitter
code: row_splitter(df, RowCount = 1500, NameVec = NULL)

title: Split a Table into N rows — row_splitter
text: df single dataframe list dataframes RowCount number rows per split dataframe. Defaults 1500 NameVec single dataframe name list dataframe names, equal length number dataframes provided df. Defaults NULL fill dataframe names generic "table_n"

title: Split a Table into N rows — row_splitter
text: list named dataframes

title: Split a Table into N rows — row_splitter
code: #a simple example with a single dataframe df <- data.frame(Name = rep("example_name", 10000), Value = rep(1, 10000)) split_dfs <- row_splitter(df, 1000, "my_df")  #an example with several dataframes a_2025 <- data.frame(Name = rep("A", 10000), Year = rep(2025, 10000), Value = rep(1, 10000)) a_2024 <- data.frame(Name = rep("A", 10000), Year = rep(2024, 10000), Value = rep(1, 10000)) b_2025 <- data.frame(Name = rep("B", 10000), Year = rep(2025, 10000), Value = rep(1, 10000)) b_2024 <- data.frame(Name = rep("B", 10000), Year = rep(2024, 10000), Value = rep(1, 10000))  df_list <- list(a_2025, a_2024, b_2025, b_2024) df_names <- list("a_2025"," a_2024", "b_2025", "b_2024")  split_dfs <- row_splitter(df_list, 1000, df_names)

title: Conditional Formatting with .xlsx Output — save_n3_table
text: df standard dataframe, data.frame, table, spreadsheet, etc. file_name String. name save output , without extension. target_columns Numeric Vector. columns colour grading applied . e.g. 1:10. Defaults . target_rows Numeric Vector. rows colour grading applied . e.g. 1:10. scheme String. One several options: Report Card, Rainfall, Temperature, Summary Statistic, Presence Absence. determines colour scheme conditional formatting schemes used. include_letter Boolean. letter grade attached score? specific Report Card scores (Report Card scheme).

title: Conditional Formatting with .xlsx Output — save_n3_table
text: .xlsx output

title: Conditional Formatting with .xlsx Output — save_n3_table
code: if (FALSE)  #dont run because the function saves an file externally  x <- data.frame(  Basin = c("Black", "Ross", "Haughton", "Suttor"),  DIN = c(51, 76, 27, 98),  TP = c(90, 57, 34, 72) ) save_n3_table(  df = x,   file_name = "final_scores",   target_columns = 2:3,   target_rows = 2:5,   scheme = "Report Card",  include_letter = FALSE ) #> Error: object 'x' not found  # \dontrun{}

what: Compare how a dataset changes over time — sf_change_over_time
title: Compare how a dataset changes over time — sf_change_over_time
text: Compare dataset changes time

title: Compare how a dataset changes over time — sf_change_over_time
code: sf_change_over_time(sf, Col, StartYear, EndYear, Resolution = 1000)

title: Compare how a dataset changes over time — sf_change_over_time
text: sf sf object. Generally object contain polygons geometries, must contain least 2 years data. Col Character string. name column extract information geometry StartYear Numeric string. first year data use. Must exist within sf object. EndYear Numeric string. second year data use. Must exist within sf object. Resolution Numeric string. number grid cells create along x y planes - square grid. Defaults 1000

title: Compare how a dataset changes over time — sf_change_over_time
text: sf object

title: Compare how a dataset changes over time — sf_change_over_time
code: polygons <- list(   rbind(c(0,0), c(1,0), c(1,1), c(0,1), c(0,0)),    rbind(c(1,0), c(2,0), c(2,1), c(1,1), c(1,0)),   rbind(c(0,1), c(1,1), c(1,2), c(0,2), c(0,1)),   rbind(c(1,1), c(2,1), c(2,2), c(1,2), c(1,1)))  polygons <- sf::st_sfc(lapply(polygons, function(x) sf::st_polygon(list(x))))  poly_sf <- dplyr::bind_rows(   sf::st_sf(Year = 2000, Id = c("a", "b", "c", "d"), geometry = polygons, crs = 4326),    sf::st_sf(Year = 2020, Id = rep("a",4), geometry = polygons, crs = 4326))  change <- sf_change_over_time(poly_sf, "Id", 2000, 2020) #> Warning: x is already of type POLYGON. #> Joining with `by = join_by(PreValue)` #> Joining with `by = join_by(PostValue)`  tmap::tm_shape(change) + tmap::tm_polygons(fill = "ValueChange")

what: Function that converts values to score — value_to_score
title: Function that converts values to score — value_to_score
text: Function converts values score

title: Function that converts values to score — value_to_score
code: value_to_score(   df,   value,   value_type,   water_type = NULL,   indicator = NULL,   wqo = NULL,   sf = NULL,   eightieth = NULL,   twentieth = NULL )

title: Function that converts values to score — value_to_score
text: df Dataframe. table contains value(s) interested . value Numeric column. One numeric column contain values scored. Values ranges can vary significantly depending indicator. value_type String. Defines type values scored. One : "Water Quality", "Pesticides", "Wetlands", "Mangroves Saltmarsh", "Riparian", "Fish". water_type String. Defines type water quality scoring use. relevant water quality. One "Freshwater", "Estuarine", "Marine" indicator String column. Defines indicator scored. relevant water quality fish. sourced df value column(s). wqo Numeric column. water quality objective associated value. relevant water quality. sourced df value column(s). sf Numeric column. scaling factor associated value. relevant freshwater estuarine water quality. sourced df value column(s). eightieth Numeric column. eightieth percentile associated value. relevant freshwater estuarine water quality. sourced df value column(s). twentieth Numeric column. twentieth percentile associated value. relevant freshwater estuarine water quality. sourced df value column(s).

title: Function that converts values to score — value_to_score
text: original dataframe new score columns added. Names inherited targeted columns.

title: Function that converts values to score — value_to_score
code: x <- data.frame(  Basin = c("Black", "Ross", "Haughton", "Suttor"),  Indicator = c("DIN", "DIN", "Low_DO", "Low_DO"),  Value = c(0.002, 0.017, 87.6, 104),  Wqo = c(0.02, 0.02, 90, 90),  Sf = c(0.38, 0.38, 70, 70),  Eightieth = c(0.0154, 0.0154, 101.12, 101.12),  Twentieth = c(0.0026, 0.0026, 75.84, 75.84) )  x <- x |>    value_to_score(     value = Value,     value_type = "Water Quality",     water_type = "Freshwater",     indicator = Indicator,     wqo = Wqo,     sf = Sf,     eightieth = Eightieth,     twentieth = Twentieth   )

title: Function that weights scores based on predetermined weighting values — weight_score
code: x <- data.frame(  Basin = c("Black", "Ross", "Haughton", "Suttor"),  Indicator = c("DIN", "DIN", "Low_DO", "Low_DO"),  Score = c(100, 55, 67, 18),  Weight = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.3, 0.2) )  x <- x |>    weight_score(     score = Score,     weighting = Weight   )
